title: Hybrid large language model approach for prompt and sensitive defect management: A comparative analysis of hybrid, non-hybrid, and GraphRAG approaches
contribution: 提出混合LLM方法在保护数据隐私的同时实现高质量缺陷问答，ROUGE得分81.6%
abstract: This study proposes a hybrid LLM-enhanced defect question-answering (QA) method that secures private and sensitive data while yielding high performance. Prompt responses to residents' complaints are crucial for preventing recurring defects. However, traditional defect analysis relies on the expertise of a few skilled workers. LLMs offer a potential solution, but many companies prohibit closed-source LLM services like ChatGPT due to data breach concerns. Open-source LLMs can be locally deployed but typically perform worse. This study proposes a hybrid method that deploys an open-source LLM (Llama 3) for defect management to secure sensitive information and a closed-source LLM (GPT-4o) for generating synthetic training datasets. The method is compared to GPT-4o, Llama 3, and GraphRAG-based QA systems. The hybrid LLM-based QA method achieved the highest ROUGE score of 81.6%.
methodology: Proposes a hybrid QA method: (1) a closed-source LLM (GPT-4o) generates synthetic QA datasets from structured defect cases via a Prompt Development Cycle (PrDC) with chain-of-thought instructions; (2) an open-source LLM (Llama 3-8B-Instruct) is fine-tuned on the synthetic dataset using QLoRA (4-bit quantization, rank 32) for on-premises deployment. The method is compared against non-hybrid approaches (Llama 3 and GPT-4o-mini fine-tuned on their own data) and GraphRAG-based QA systems using a knowledge graph with 10 node labels and 11 relationship types built from the same 247 defect cases. Evaluation uses ROUGE, F1 Score, BERT Score, and SBERT Score.
results: The hybrid method (Llama 3 fine-tuned on GPT-4o-generated data) achieved the best performance with ROUGE 81.6%, F1 Score 80.2%, and BERT Score 0.785, significantly outperforming non-hybrid fine-tuning and GraphRAG approaches. GraphRAG-based QA performed poorly due to token-level metric limitations and primary node retrieval quality issues (best SBERT score 0.525). Synthetic QA dataset generation required less than $20 and under 1 hour, compared to months for traditional manual construction.
